title: podman 3.0.1 network - partially dropping network traffic for some hosts
body:
How can I debug a situation where the tcp handshake is working and https traffic is working partially for some hosts?

Situation:

partially traffic between ipv4 internet and a podman container(f.e alpine) is dropped.

 * https://www.google.com is working
 * https://s3.eu-central-003.backblazeb2.com is not working
 * tcp handshake is working in container and host network

hardware setup:
 - router01
   - hosted on hetzner
   - firewall outbound traffic allow all
   - firewall inbound traffic allow only ssh and icmp
   - ip4.forwarding enabled
   - public ipv6
   - public ipv4
   - private ipv4
   - masquerade enabled
 - podman-host
   - hosted on hetzner
   - firewall outbound traffic allow all
   - firewall inbound traffic allow only ssh and icmp
   - ip4.forwarding enabled
   - public ipv6
   - private ipv4
   - ipv4 default route via router01

podman details:
```
podman -v
podman version 3.0.1
 podman info
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.19.6
  cgroupManager: systemd
  cgroupVersion: v2
  conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/bin/conmon'
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.25, commit: unknown'
  cpus: 2
  distribution:
    distribution: debian
    version: "11"
  eventLogger: journald
  hostname: mgmt03
  idMappings:
    gidmap: null
    uidmap: null
  kernel: 5.10.0-18-amd64
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 478965760
  memTotal: 2028859392
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: 'crun: /usr/bin/crun'
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.17
      commit: 0e9229ae34caaebcb86f1fde18de3acaf18c6d9a
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    exists: true
    path: /run/podman/podman.sock
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: false
    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: false
    seccompEnabled: true
    selinuxEnabled: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: ""
    package: ""
    version: ""
  swapFree: 0
  swapTotal: 0
  uptime: 2h 33m 16.64s (Approximately 0.08 days)
registries: {}
store:
  configFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 4
    paused: 0
    running: 3
    stopped: 1
  graphDriverName: overlay
  graphOptions: {}
  graphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  imageStore:
    number: 6
  runRoot: /run/containers/storage
  volumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
version:
  APIVersion: 3.0.0
  Built: 0
  BuiltTime: Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
  GitCommit: ""
  GoVersion: go1.15.15
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 3.0.1
```

starting a alpine container:
```
podman run --rm -d -t alpine /bin/sh
podman inspect -l | grep -i image
        "Image": "9c6f0724472873bb50a2ae67a9e7adcb57673a183cea8b06eb778dca859181b5",
        "ImageName": "docker.io/library/alpine:latest",
            "Image": "docker.io/library/alpine:latest",
                "org.opencontainers.image.stopSignal": "15"
podman inspect -l | grep -i pid
            "Pid": 8000,
            "ConmonPid": 7997,
        "ConmonPidFile": "/run/containers/storage/overlay-containers/023b9d252f9a8a4f8701f43d63df8200411cda7b72b945f347ed35b58227dacf/userdata/conmon.pid",
            "PidMode": "private",
            "PidsLimit": 2048,
```

First I tried if all tcp connections are dropped:
host:
```
nc -z 45.11.37.254 443 && echo $?
0
```

container:
```
nsenter -t 8000 -n -- nc -z 45.11.37.254 443 && echo $?
0
```

TCP handshake is working. Afterwards I tried google to verify internet is working with ipv4:

host:
```
curl -s -o /dev/null -4 -v https://www.google.com                                                                                                                                                   
*   Trying 142.250.186.132:443...                                                                        
* Connected to www.google.com (142.250.186.132) port 443 (#0)                                            
* ALPN, offering h2                     
* ALPN, offering http/1.1                                                                                
* successfully set certificate verify locations:                                                         
*  CAfile: /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt                                                            
*  CApath: /etc/ssl/certs                        
} [5 bytes data]                       
* TLSv1.3 (OUT), TLS handshake, Client hello (1):
} [512 bytes data]                                                                                                                                                                                                 
* TLSv1.3 (IN), TLS handshake, Server hello (2):                                                                                                                                                                   
{ [122 bytes data]                                                                                       
* TLSv1.3 (IN), TLS handshake, Encrypted Extensions (8):                                                                                                                                                           
{ [15 bytes data]                                                                                                                                                                                                  
* TLSv1.3 (IN), TLS handshake, Certificate (11):                                                         
{ [4003 bytes data]    
* TLSv1.3 (IN), TLS handshake, CERT verify (15):                                                         
{ [78 bytes data] 
* TLSv1.3 (IN), TLS handshake, Finished (20):     
{ [52 bytes data]                                                                                                                                                                                                  
* TLSv1.3 (OUT), TLS change cipher, Change cipher spec (1):                                              
} [1 bytes data]                                                                                         
* TLSv1.3 (OUT), TLS handshake, Finished (20):                                                                                                                                                                     
} [52 bytes data]                                                                                        
* SSL connection using TLSv1.3 / TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384
* ALPN, server accepted to use h2  
* Server certificate:                                                                                    
*  subject: CN=www.google.com                                                                            
*  start date: Sep 12 08:19:33 2022 GMT
*  expire date: Dec  5 08:19:32 2022 GMT
*  subjectAltName: host "www.google.com" matched cert's "www.google.com"
*  issuer: C=US; O=Google Trust Services LLC; CN=GTS CA 1C3                                                                                                                                                        
*  SSL certificate verify ok.
```

container:
```
nsenter -t 8000 -n -- curl --resolve "www.google.com:443:142.250.186.132" -s -o /dev/null -4 -v https://www.google.com
* Added www.google.com:443:142.250.186.132 to DNS cache    
* Hostname www.google.com was found in DNS cache
*   Trying 142.250.186.132:443...
* Connected to www.google.com (142.250.186.132) port 443 (#0)
* ALPN, offering h2
* ALPN, offering http/1.1          
* successfully set certificate verify locations:
*  CAfile: /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt                                                            
*  CApath: /etc/ssl/certs
} [5 bytes data]     
* TLSv1.3 (OUT), TLS handshake, Client hello (1):
} [512 bytes data]                                                                                                                                                                                                 
* TLSv1.3 (IN), TLS handshake, Server hello (2):                                                                                                                                                                   { [122 bytes data]                                                                                       
* TLSv1.3 (IN), TLS handshake, Encrypted Extensions (8):                                                                                                                                                           
{ [15 bytes data]                                                                                                                                                                                                  
* TLSv1.3 (IN), TLS handshake, Certificate (11):
{ [4003 bytes data]    
* TLSv1.3 (IN), TLS handshake, CERT verify (15):
{ [78 bytes data]
* TLSv1.3 (IN), TLS handshake, Finished (20):     
{ [52 bytes data]
* TLSv1.3 (OUT), TLS change cipher, Change cipher spec (1):
} [1 bytes data]
* TLSv1.3 (OUT), TLS handshake, Finished (20):
} [52 bytes data]
* SSL connection using TLSv1.3 / TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384
* ALPN, server accepted to use h2
* Server certificate:
*  subject: CN=www.google.com
*  start date: Sep 12 08:19:33 2022 GMT
*  expire date: Dec  5 08:19:32 2022 GMT
*  subjectAltName: host "www.google.com" matched cert's "www.google.com"
*  issuer: C=US; O=Google Trust Services LLC; CN=GTS CA 1C3
*  SSL certificate verify ok.
```

Google is working and https is also working as expected. Now I tried exactly the same with backblaze:

host:
```
curl -s -o /dev/null -4 -v https://s3.eu-central-003.backblazeb2.com
 *   Trying 45.11.37.254:443...
 * Connected to s3.eu-central-003.backblazeb2.com (45.11.37.254) port 443 (#0)
 * ALPN, offering h2
 * ALPN, offering http/1.1
 * successfully set certificate verify locations:
 *  CAfile: /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt
 *  CApath: /etc/ssl/certs
} [5 bytes data]
 * TLSv1.3 (OUT), TLS handshake, Client hello (1):
} [512 bytes data]
 * TLSv1.3 (IN), TLS handshake, Server hello (2):
{ [122 bytes data]
 * TLSv1.3 (IN), TLS handshake, Encrypted Extensions (8):
{ [32 bytes data]
 * TLSv1.3 (IN), TLS handshake, Certificate (11):
{ [4392 bytes data]
 * TLSv1.3 (IN), TLS handshake, CERT verify (15):
{ [264 bytes data]
 * TLSv1.3 (IN), TLS handshake, Finished (20):
{ [36 bytes data]
 * TLSv1.3 (OUT), TLS change cipher, Change cipher spec (1):
} [1 bytes data]
 * TLSv1.3 (OUT), TLS handshake, Finished (20):
} [36 bytes data]
 * SSL connection using TLSv1.3 / TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256
 * ALPN, server did not agree to a protocol
 * Server certificate:
 *  subject: CN=backblazeb2.com
 *  start date: Sep 26 21:35:51 2022 GMT
 *  expire date: Dec 25 21:35:50 2022 GMT
 *  subjectAltName: host "s3.eu-central-003.backblazeb2.com" matched cert's "s3.eu-central-003.backblazeb2.com"
 *  issuer: C=US; O=Let's Encrypt; CN=R3
 *  SSL certificate verify ok.
```

container(command is slightly different, I added a timeout to not wait ages):
```
nsenter -t 8000 -n -- curl --connect-timeout 2 --resolve "s3.eu-central-003.backblazeb2.com:443:45.11.37.254" -s -o /dev/null -4 -v https://s3.eu-central-003.backblazeb2.com
 * Added s3.eu-central-003.backblazeb2.com:443:45.11.37.254 to DNS cache
 * Hostname s3.eu-central-003.backblazeb2.com was found in DNS cache
 *   Trying 45.11.37.254:443...
 * Connected to s3.eu-central-003.backblazeb2.com (45.11.37.254) port 443 (#0)
 * ALPN, offering h2
 * ALPN, offering http/1.1
 * successfully set certificate verify locations:
 *  CAfile: /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt
 *  CApath: /etc/ssl/certs
} [5 bytes data]
 * TLSv1.3 (OUT), TLS handshake, Client hello (1):
} [512 bytes data]
 * TLSv1.3 (IN), TLS handshake, Server hello (2):
{ [122 bytes data]
 * Operation timed out after 2001 milliseconds with 0 out of 0 bytes received
 * Closing connection 0
```


Handshake is working, SSL Hello is received but more data is dropped.

Everything I came up didn't hit, now I need some help to understand what is going on here. I tried a reboot, I tried another host, I tried another container, I tried to disable all firewalls...

comments.body:
We only support the latest versions upstream and 3.0 is super old.
Either way this looks like generic network issue and not something podman is doing.

3.0.1 is the latest in debian stable https://packages.debian.org/search?keywords=podman

I'll try to update it to 3.4.7.
